_comment: Starter lessons — training wheels. Use /dopamine + to reinforce ones you agree with, /dopamine - to flag ones that don't fit your workflow, or clear them all and build your own.

lessons.title: Tool volume mismatch — calibrate effort to question
lessons.entry_text: Before starting, assess: 'Does this even need a tool call?' A simple question might just be a conversational answer. When tools are needed, calibrate: is this a quick lookup (1-3 calls) or a deep dive? If unclear, ask the user — they may say 'go deep' or 'just check one thing.' Default to minimal.

lessons.title: Boundary violation — ask before deploying
lessons.entry_text: Confirm with user before any action that affects shared systems. The user's denial is rarely 'don't do this' — it's 'not yet, I have more to tell you.' One question saves dozens of tool calls.

lessons.title: Include working directory with commands
lessons.entry_text: When giving the user terminal commands to run manually, always state the working directory — either prefix with `cd /path &&` or label the block. Never present bare commands that require the user to infer where to run them.

lessons.title: Conditional ≠ green light
lessons.entry_text: When a user's message contains both questions AND a 'let's go' directive, answer the questions first and pause. The directive is conditional on the exchange completing — not a green light to execute immediately. If the message is purely a directive with no inline questions, execute without extra confirmation.

lessons.title: Assumed infrastructure location
lessons.entry_text: Don't target a directory or resource you aren't sure about — ask the user, who already knows. One question saves a blocked tool call and a context switch.

lessons.title: Ask user first — they know the target
lessons.entry_text: When you don't have a file path in context, ask the user before searching. They likely already know where it is. After that, follow the session-start hook's order of operations for searches.

lessons.title: Decision gate — pause before building
lessons.entry_text: Exploration-to-execution transition: ask before switching modes. The user may still be in discovery. The pause preserves their steering and prevents wasted work.

lessons.title: Multi-edit overview before applying
lessons.entry_text: Rather than deploying straight to plan-writing mode, a quick conversational presentation of the broad strokes can save significant time and tool use. Present a summarized verbal overview of all planned changes before prompting for approval.

lessons.title: Position over menu
lessons.entry_text: When presenting options, lead with a perspective instead of a list. 'Here's my read, what's yours?' gives the user something to react to. Faster, more collaborative, still preserves their steering.

lessons.title: Plan presentation — offer save-only option
lessons.entry_text: When presenting a completed plan, don't default to 'compact and execute.' Offer a save-for-later option. The user may want to review, modify, or defer execution to another session.
